package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Emergency Severity Index (ESI): A Triage Tool for Emergency Department Care, Version 4
topic_category_or_theme: red flag missed; normal-test trap; incomplete history
finding_or_quote_or_case: Triage is a rapid risk sort, not a diagnosis; subtle chronic or complex symptoms may appear low acuity if vitals are normal.
what_happened: Classify acuity using ESI: immediate life-saving intervention, high risk/severe pain, then expected resources, with danger-zone vitals review.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: patients needing immediate lifesaving intervention and high-risk care; risk: triage depends on nurse pattern recognition and local resource assumptions.
product_or_system_implication: Separate emergency risk score from diagnostic uncertainty; capture clinician concern and prior visits even when ESI level is lower.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Diagnostic Errors in the Emergency Department: A Systematic Review
patient_stage_or_process_step: first clinician assessment; imaging/lab interpretation; discharge
condition_or_issue: stroke, MI, aortic dissection, VTE, meningitis/encephalitis, sepsis, spinal cord injury/compression, lung cancer
topic_category_or_theme: red flag missed; repeated visits ignored; no follow-up ownership
finding_or_quote_or_case: High-risk diagnoses may be missed at the first visit and only recognized after repeat ED return or deterioration.
what_happened: Systematic review estimated diagnostic error harms and common high-harm conditions.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Missed/delayed: serious ED diagnoses, especially vascular, neurologic, infection and cancer presentations.
product_or_system_implication: Flag high-harm conditions linked to symptom clusters and require explicit exclusion/safety-netting before discharge.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Guidance for emergency departments: initial assessment
patient_stage_or_process_step: arrival; registration; nurse triage; streaming; first clinician assessment
topic_category_or_theme: poor handoff; red flag missed; no follow-up ownership
finding_or_quote_or_case: Patients may experience an 'occult wait' before triage if registration queues or digital check-in create hidden delays.
what_happened: Initial assessment can include streaming, triage and rapid assessment/treatment to identify sickest patients and direct others to right service.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: sickest patients and appropriate service route; risk: redirection decisions can become unsafe if not clinically governed.
product_or_system_implication: Track real elapsed time from physical arrival to clinical assessment, not just time after registration.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Initial Assessment of Emergency Department Patients
topic_category_or_theme: arrival; registration; red flag missed; poor handoff
finding_or_quote_or_case: Operational queues can hide clinical risk before the patient is visible to the triage system.
what_happened: Register quickly, perform initial assessment by trained registered clinician, record minimum clinical fields and use early warning scores where relevant.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: front-door risk; missed risk if queues are unmanaged before registration/assessment.
product_or_system_implication: Use queue surveillance and arrival-time capture; include deterioration prompts before formal triage is complete.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Emergency Department Workflow Diagrams
patient_stage_or_process_step: arrival; intake; labs ordered; imaging ordered; lab interpretation; discharge/admission
topic_category_or_theme: documentation error; no one connected the dots; normal-test trap
finding_or_quote_or_case: Decision support needs upstream data completeness and downstream reassessment when diagnosis does not fit.
what_happened: Patient arrives by EMS/walk-in; triage/intake RN collects key data; provider orders labs/CXR; CURB-65 and clinical assessment guide admission vs outpatient antibiotics.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: pneumonia severity and disposition; risk: pneumonia mimic or alternative diagnosis may be missed if CDS anchors on CAP.
product_or_system_implication: Expose missing CURB-65 elements and create 'pursue other diagnoses' tracking, not just CAP disposition.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Urgent care self-service
topic_category_or_theme: digital access gap; incomplete history; red flag missed
finding_or_quote_or_case: Self-service can speed flow but may miss nuance, language barriers or worsening while waiting.
what_happened: Patient self-checks in on kiosk/tablet, completes symptom triage, and can be prioritised, redirected or referred through service routing.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: rapid symptom-led prioritisation and redirection; risk: digital access, literacy and symptom-description gaps.
product_or_system_implication: Add accessibility fallback, clinician override, real-time deterioration alerts and visibility of prior visits.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Urgent treatment centres: principles and standards
patient_stage_or_process_step: arrival; first clinician assessment; labs ordered; imaging ordered; referral; follow-up
condition_or_issue: minor injuries/ailments and urgent non-life-threatening problems
topic_category_or_theme: wrong specialty; poor handoff; no follow-up ownership
finding_or_quote_or_case: UTC boundaries are porous; patients may need escalation, diagnostics or follow-up ownership outside the UTC.
what_happened: UTCs accept booked and walk-in patients; provide basic diagnostics or protocols; share records/post-event messages and refer to ED/specialists when needed.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: conditions manageable outside ED; risk: lack of diagnostic capability or wrong redirection.
product_or_system_implication: Show available diagnostics, escalation criteria and post-visit ownership in the same workflow.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: When to visit an urgent treatment centre
patient_stage_or_process_step: arrival; vitals; first clinician assessment; discharge/referral
topic_category_or_theme: red flag missed; patient self-advocacy required
finding_or_quote_or_case: The workflow depends on patients knowing when and how to report deterioration while waiting.
what_happened: On arrival, patients may have observations by nurse, may wait by acuity not arrival order, and can be assessed/treated onsite or referred; GP is updated afterwards.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: worsening symptoms during wait if patient tells staff; risk: passive reliance on patient to report deterioration.
product_or_system_implication: Provide waiting-room check-ins and explicit 'tell us now if...' symptom prompts in multiple languages.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Vital Signs in the Urgent Care Center
patient_stage_or_process_step: vitals; nurse triage; first clinician assessment
topic_category_or_theme: normal-test trap; lab trend missed; documentation error
finding_or_quote_or_case: Vitals create early signal but can be incomplete, delayed or not trended against prior visits.
what_happened: Initial vitals tailored to age, complaint and status; abnormal vitals reported and repeated.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: physiologic instability; missed: serious disease with normal or incomplete vitals.
product_or_system_implication: Require abnormal-vital acknowledgment, repeat-vital timers and trend comparison across visits.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Red Flags Identification and Intervention Policy for Urgent Care Centers
condition_or_issue: emergency red-flag presentations presenting to urgent care
topic_category_or_theme: red flag missed; incomplete history; poor handoff
finding_or_quote_or_case: Non-clinical intake staff may be the first safety barrier.
what_happened: Registration/intake staff ask reason for visit; red flags trigger immediate rooming and clinician involvement or ED transfer.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: conditions too high-risk for routine urgent care; missed: red flags hidden by vague complaint or poor intake phrasing.
product_or_system_implication: Use plain-language red-flag checklist and escalation button at registration.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: NHS 111 online
topic_category_or_theme: incomplete history; wrong specialty; no one connected the dots
finding_or_quote_or_case: Digital triage may fragment complex histories into a single main symptom.
what_happened: Patient completes one symptom pathway; service gives advice such as self-care, pharmacist, GP, UTC, ED or callback.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: service disposition; missed: multi-symptom or known-condition context if algorithm is used one symptom at a time.
product_or_system_implication: Allow multi-symptom summaries, 'not captured' text, prior-risk integration and easy escalation to human review.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: NHS 111 online staff information
topic_category_or_theme: wrong specialty; poor handoff; fragmented care
finding_or_quote_or_case: The handoff is only as good as the service directory and referral visibility.
what_happened: Uses structured triage and service directory to recommend ambulance/ED/UTC/out-of-hours GP/GP/pharmacist/self-care outcomes.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: appropriate service destination; missed: directory availability or local pathway mismatches can redirect incorrectly.
product_or_system_implication: Audit outcome-to-arrival completion and failed referral attempts; surface no-capacity conditions.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Digital tools for online consultation in general practice
patient_stage_or_process_step: before visit; intake form; first clinician assessment; follow-up
condition_or_issue: varied primary-care requests; hidden serious illness
topic_category_or_theme: digital access gap; incomplete history; no follow-up ownership; chronic illness invalidation
finding_or_quote_or_case: Online access can create a digital bottleneck and reduce the chance to observe/examine patients.
what_happened: Asynchronous online consultations support clinical/admin requests but may be capped or rerouted by practices for safety.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Missed/delayed: safety incidents when digital tools reduce access, distort history or weaken proactive/long-term care.
product_or_system_implication: Create safe fallback channels, complexity flags, remote-to-face-to-face conversion rules and continuity prompts.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Telehealth and Patient Safety
patient_stage_or_process_step: medical history; physical exam; vitals; referral; follow-up
condition_or_issue: acute or chronic problems assessed remotely
topic_category_or_theme: normal-test trap; incomplete physical exam; poor follow-up ownership
finding_or_quote_or_case: Telehealth changes the signal set available for diagnosis.
what_happened: Telehealth can be safer when embedded in a medical home with records, tests, referrals and follow-up; risk rises when exam and communication are limited.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Missed: diagnostic cues requiring auscultation, private/inaccessible body areas, objective vitals or hands-on exam.
product_or_system_implication: Decision support should classify problems as telehealth-suitable vs requiring in-person exam/labs/imaging.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Modern General Practice Model
patient_stage_or_process_step: before visit; intake form; care navigation; first clinician assessment
topic_category_or_theme: incomplete history; wrong specialty; no one connected the dots
finding_or_quote_or_case: Routing can optimize capacity but also become a diagnostic gate if symptoms are under-described.
what_happened: Modern access model directs patients via phone/online/in-person to the right professional first time, using care navigation and demand/capacity management.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: routing need; missed: clinical complexity if navigation lacks enough symptom/risk context.
product_or_system_implication: Separate admin routing from clinical triage; capture escalation flags and repeated contacts.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Care navigation and triage in general practice
topic_category_or_theme: poor handoff; symptom dismissed; red flag missed
finding_or_quote_or_case: Ambiguity over whether a contact is administrative or clinical can lose risk signals.
what_happened: Care navigation signposts to appropriate clinician or service; clinical triage by nurse/GP makes early clinical decisions.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: difference between signposting and clinical triage; missed: escalation if administrative navigation substitutes for clinical judgment.
product_or_system_implication: Make role boundaries explicit and route uncertain cases to clinical triage.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Suspected cancer: recognition and referral (NG12)
patient_stage_or_process_step: medical history; physical exam; labs ordered; referral; safety netting
topic_category_or_theme: normal-test trap; referral delay; rare disease delay
finding_or_quote_or_case: Threshold pathways may leave low-risk but persistent symptoms in a monitoring black zone.
what_happened: Guideline organizes referral/investigation actions by cancer site, symptom and findings; includes safety netting and diagnostic-process advice.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: cancer suspicion thresholds; missed: patients below threshold or with nonspecific/vague symptoms unless active monitoring occurs.
product_or_system_implication: Add active-monitoring tasks and symptom persistence triggers, not only referral thresholds.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Safety netting
condition_or_issue: possible cancer or other unresolved condition
topic_category_or_theme: normal-test trap; no follow-up ownership; repeated visits ignored
finding_or_quote_or_case: A negative test can prematurely close a diagnostic episode.
what_happened: Safety-netting gives time-bound follow-up, documents advice, ensures tests/referrals completed and monitors persistent symptoms even after negative tests.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: ongoing risk despite low-risk or negative initial results; missed: no owner for follow-up tasks.
product_or_system_implication: Build safety-net plans with due dates, owner, patient-facing instructions and re-presentation counters.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: GP mythbuster: managing test results in general practice
topic_category_or_theme: abnormal result not followed up; documentation error; no follow-up ownership
finding_or_quote_or_case: Test-result management crosses many people and organisations.
what_happened: Practice needs systems for ordering, tracking attendance, return/non-return, review, filing, patient communication and abnormal follow-up.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Missed: abnormal result not acted on, result not returned, no patient communication or clinician absence gap.
product_or_system_implication: Implement closed-loop result tracking, unresolved-result inboxes and backup reviewer rules.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Continuity of care: delayed diagnosis in GP practices
patient_stage_or_process_step: repeat visit; medical history; specialist review; emergency escalation
condition_or_issue: metastatic breast cancer presenting as back pain
topic_category_or_theme: fragmented care; no one connected the dots; missing family/history context; repeated visits ignored
finding_or_quote_or_case: No one clinician/system connected the repeated back-pain presentations with prior cancer risk.
what_happened: Over 8 months the patient saw many clinicians; eventual GP found spinal lump and sent patient to ED where CT found metastatic cancer.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Missed/delayed: unresolved symptoms and cancer-history context were not surfaced across repeat contacts.
product_or_system_implication: Surface unresolved symptom threads, prior high-risk history and multiple-clinician patterns in the EHR.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Jess's Rule: Three strikes and we rethink
patient_stage_or_process_step: repeat visit; physical exam; referral; labs ordered
condition_or_issue: persistent or escalating unexplained symptoms; cancer in origin story
topic_category_or_theme: repeated visits ignored; no one connected the dots; bias or minimization
finding_or_quote_or_case: Repeat visits are often visible only as isolated encounters.
what_happened: After three presentations, GP teams are prompted to reflect, review and rethink, consider face-to-face exam, second opinion, tests or referral.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: repeat presentation as a diagnostic signal; missed: if repeat visits are not counted across clinicians/channels.
product_or_system_implication: Create automatic repeat-presentation counters across phone/online/in-person and linked symptoms.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Years of being dismissed, ignored and belittled: Endometriosis UK diagnosis report
patient_stage_or_process_step: medical history; referral; specialist review; repeat visit
topic_category_or_theme: symptom dismissed; chronic illness invalidation; bias or minimization; patient self-advocacy required
finding_or_quote_or_case: Chronic pain and women's health symptoms can be minimized despite repeated presentations.
what_happened: Survey documented repeated GP visits and A&E attendance before diagnosis; respondents reported dismissal/minimisation.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Missed/delayed: endometriosis diagnosis; patients often required repeated self-advocacy.
product_or_system_implication: Track symptom duration/severity, repeated visits and invalidating language; prompt specialist referral criteria review.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: The impact of waiting for care
topic_category_or_theme: referral delay; no follow-up ownership; patient self-advocacy required
finding_or_quote_or_case: Referral is not a completed action unless the patient is seen, tests are done and results acted on.
what_happened: Healthwatch gathered patient reports of waiting after GP referral, confusion over results and pressure to chase.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Missed/delayed: follow-up appointments, specialist action, scans/tests; GP sometimes unable to accelerate despite abnormal tests.
product_or_system_implication: Track referral state, wait risk, abnormal-test escalation and patient-visible next step.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Patients being harmed due to repeated mistakes in reading scans
patient_stage_or_process_step: imaging ordered; lab interpretation; follow-up; emergency return
topic_category_or_theme: abnormal result not followed up; red flag missed; repeated visits ignored
finding_or_quote_or_case: Imaging workflows fail at multiple points: ordering, performance, interpretation, communication, follow-up.
what_happened: Ombudsman reported repeated failures in scan reading, carrying out scans and follow-up; a cancer case was only identified at a fifth A&E visit after lesion not reported.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Missed/delayed: abnormal scan findings and cancer diagnosis.
product_or_system_implication: Closed-loop imaging: critical finding acknowledgment, patient notification and escalation if no action documented.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Failures in communication or follow-up of unexpected significant radiological findings
patient_stage_or_process_step: imaging ordered; imaging interpretation; discharge; follow-up
condition_or_issue: lung cancer / unexpected significant radiology finding
topic_category_or_theme: poor handoff; abnormal result not followed up; no follow-up ownership
finding_or_quote_or_case: Responsibility can diffuse between radiology, ED, GP and patient.
what_happened: Investigation traced multistep communication of unexpected significant findings and weak assurance that action occurred.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Missed/delayed: significant finding not received or acted on; read receipts not enough.
product_or_system_implication: Require an accountable result owner, escalation timer and auditable action completion, not just report delivery.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Core Elements of Hospital Diagnostic Excellence
condition_or_issue: missed diagnoses after discharge; lung malignancy suspicion; pending tests
topic_category_or_theme: poor handoff; no follow-up ownership; abnormal result not followed up
finding_or_quote_or_case: Discharge can sever ownership of pending results.
what_happened: Hospital diagnostic excellence framework encourages targeting pending tests and suspicious imaging follow-up.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: high-risk diagnostic follow-up areas; missed: patients leaving hospital before test closure.
product_or_system_implication: Create discharge-pending-test registry and patient/clinician notification loops.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Referral Assessment Services - NHS e-Referral Service
condition_or_issue: conditions needing specialist opinion or diagnostic pathway
topic_category_or_theme: wrong specialty; referral delay; poor handoff
finding_or_quote_or_case: Referral triage creates a new work queue where responsibility can be unclear to patient and referrer.
what_happened: Referral Assessment Services allow provider review before booking; outcomes include booking, redirecting, requesting more info, or returning advice.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: suitable pathway before appointment; missed: wrong worklist/status or insufficient referral information delays care.
product_or_system_implication: Make referral state and owner visible to referrer and patient; alert when more information is requested or referral returned.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Diagnostic services - NHS e-Referral Service
patient_stage_or_process_step: referral; imaging ordered; labs ordered; specialist review
condition_or_issue: conditions suitable for direct-to-test or clinical assessment
topic_category_or_theme: incomplete history; wrong specialty; documentation error
finding_or_quote_or_case: Straight-to-test pathways are efficient only when referral information is complete.
what_happened: Diagnostic e-RS models include direct-to-test, clinical assessment then test, and one-stop consultant-led models.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: bypassing first outpatient appointment for needed tests; missed: poor referral templates can omit critical context.
product_or_system_implication: Embed mandatory clinical context fields and feedback loops for rejected/inappropriate tests.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Outpatient recovery and transformation improvement guide
patient_stage_or_process_step: referral; specialist review; imaging/labs ordered; follow-up
topic_category_or_theme: referral delay; no follow-up ownership; normal-test trap
finding_or_quote_or_case: Advice can reduce unnecessary appointments but must not become a soft rejection of unresolved symptoms.
what_happened: Improvement guide promotes advice/guidance, straight-to-test, pre-appointment hubs; one GI case cut waits through advice and e-RS.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: faster specialist input; missed: advice-only management without clear follow-up if symptoms persist.
product_or_system_implication: Record advice decisions with safety-net tasks and escalation criteria.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Health Center Program Compliance Manual: Continuity of Care and Hospital Admitting
condition_or_issue: post-ED/hospital follow-up and ongoing primary-care needs
topic_category_or_theme: poor handoff; fragmented care; no follow-up ownership
finding_or_quote_or_case: Safety-net clinics may be responsible for follow-up despite care occurring elsewhere.
what_happened: Health centers must have referral/hospital arrangements and processes to receive/record ED or hospital visit information and follow-up actions.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: need for continuity; missed: discharge info not received or tracked.
product_or_system_implication: Ingest outside discharge/test data and assign follow-up ownership automatically.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Health Center Program: Patient-Centered Medical Home
finding_or_quote_or_case: Coordination is a model, not a closed-loop diagnostic guarantee.
what_happened: PCMH recognition emphasizes care coordination and continuous quality improvement in health centers.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: coordination model; missed: PCMH standards may not guarantee specific diagnostic follow-up closure without local systems.
product_or_system_implication: Translate PCMH concepts into concrete diagnostic queues, unresolved-symptom lists and abnormal-result registries.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Reach and effectiveness of a centralized navigation program for patients with positive FITs
setting_or_context: community health center / public health screening
patient_stage_or_process_step: public health screening; lab interpretation; referral; follow-up
condition_or_issue: colorectal cancer risk after abnormal FIT
topic_category_or_theme: abnormal result not followed up; patient self-advocacy required; no follow-up ownership
finding_or_quote_or_case: Abnormal screening is only useful if diagnostic colonoscopy happens.
what_happened: Mailed FIT outreach identified abnormal results; centralized patient navigation addressed informational, emotional, financial and transport barriers to colonoscopy.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: abnormal FIT needing colonoscopy; missed: patients not reached or declining navigation remain at risk.
product_or_system_implication: Track abnormal-screen closure and navigator barrier categories to completion.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Implementing a successful patient navigation program for follow-up colonoscopy after abnormal FIT
topic_category_or_theme: referral delay; fragmented care; documentation error
finding_or_quote_or_case: Navigation fails if it is not staffed, documented, partnered and tracked.
what_happened: PRECISE trial implementation support trained navigators and documented challenges in workload, documentation, partnerships and tracking.
what_was_missed_or_identified: unknown
product_or_system_implication: Build navigator work queues, cross-organization data sharing and colonoscopy-status reconciliation.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Bowel cancer screening
patient_stage_or_process_step: before visit; public health screening; lab interpretation; referral; follow-up
finding_or_quote_or_case: Screening can create false reassurance if symptoms are separated from screening result interpretation.
what_happened: Eligible people receive FIT at home; results usually within 2 weeks; further tests lead to specialist nurse and usually colonoscopy; negative screen does not rule out cancer if symptoms occur.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: occult blood needing diagnostic work-up; missed: symptomatic people reassured by negative screening result.
product_or_system_implication: Pair negative results with symptom-trigger advice and GP follow-up prompts.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Breast screening programme: pathway requirements
patient_stage_or_process_step: before visit; public health screening; imaging ordered; imaging interpretation; specialist review
topic_category_or_theme: no follow-up ownership; abnormal result not followed up; patient self-advocacy required
finding_or_quote_or_case: Population screening has a built-in non-attendance black zone.
what_happened: Programme sends invitations, performs mammography, recalls non-attenders/technical failures, and sends abnormal cases to assessment clinic/MDT.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: mammographic abnormalities; missed: non-attendance, unclear images, communication failures.
product_or_system_implication: Track invitations to attendance to result to assessment closure, including failed contacts.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Patient Navigation
condition_or_issue: breast, cervical, colorectal cancer screening and diagnosis
topic_category_or_theme: fragmented care; patient self-advocacy required; referral delay
finding_or_quote_or_case: The problem is often not clinical detection but completion of the next step.
what_happened: CDC describes navigators helping people overcome practical, informational and trust barriers to screening and diagnosis.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: access barriers; missed: patients who disappear between positive screen and diagnostic test.
product_or_system_implication: Barrier taxonomy should be part of follow-up workflow, not free-text only.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Ayushman Bharat: Comprehensive Primary Health Care through Health and Wellness Centres
patient_stage_or_process_step: before visit; home/community visit; public health screening; referral; long-term monitoring
condition_or_issue: hypertension, diabetes, oral/breast/cervical cancer, mental health, child illness and broader PHC
topic_category_or_theme: referral delay; no follow-up ownership; lab trend missed
finding_or_quote_or_case: Community screening creates many positive signals that need tracking beyond the first contact.
what_happened: HWCs use empanelment, home/community interaction and universal screening to identify risks before clinic attendance; CHO leads HWC team and referrals.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: hidden NCD/cancer risk; missed: follow-up/compliance if referral loop is not closed.
product_or_system_implication: Use community registry, positive-screen worklist and two-way referral closure with ASHA follow-up.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Integrated Management of Childhood Illness
patient_stage_or_process_step: medical history; physical exam; referral; emergency escalation
condition_or_issue: childhood illnesses including pneumonia, diarrhoea, malaria, malnutrition
topic_category_or_theme: red flag missed; referral delay; incomplete history
finding_or_quote_or_case: Low-resource workflows depend heavily on algorithms and referral availability.
what_happened: IMCI uses evidence-based algorithms based on history/signs/symptoms when lab/radiology support is limited; includes treatment, counselling and referral for severe illness.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: severe illness and common syndromes without diagnostics; missed: atypical illness not covered by algorithm or supply/referral gaps.
product_or_system_implication: Show algorithm branch, missing supplies/tests and referral transport feasibility as workflow state.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Integrated Community Case Management (iCCM)
patient_stage_or_process_step: before visit; home/community visit; emergency escalation
condition_or_issue: malaria, pneumonia, diarrhoea in children under five
topic_category_or_theme: referral delay; poor handoff; low-resource supply gap
finding_or_quote_or_case: The first diagnostic encounter may happen at home, not a clinic.
what_happened: iCCM trains/supplies CHWs to diagnose/classify/treat uncomplicated cases and refer severe illness.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: common child illnesses outside facilities; missed: severe cases if CHW stockout, training gaps or referral failure.
product_or_system_implication: Design offline-first CHW decision support with danger-sign escalation and referral feedback.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Community-based case management: the RAcE programme
condition_or_issue: malaria, pneumonia, diarrhoea in under-five children
topic_category_or_theme: poor handoff; no follow-up ownership; fragmented care
finding_or_quote_or_case: Community programmes can generate care access but require supervision and integration with facilities.
what_happened: RAcE supported iCCM scale-up through CHW training, supplies and diagnosis/treatment/referral in five countries.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: millions of common illness episodes; missed: sustainability, supervision and integration challenges.
product_or_system_implication: Add supervision, stock, referral and outcome data to CHW workflows.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Effectiveness of a structured triage system in a resource-limited rural hospital in Uganda
patient_stage_or_process_step: arrival; nurse triage; vitals; first clinician assessment
condition_or_issue: urgent/emergency cases among general OPD patients
topic_category_or_theme: red flag missed; arrival; poor handoff
finding_or_quote_or_case: In many rural OPDs, primary and emergency care share a front door with no formal triage.
what_happened: Rural OPD implemented IITT-adapted structured triage using a three-colour checklist on charts and short training; patient flow improved without added resources.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: urgent/emergency cases earlier; missed: undertriage still possible and formal triage requires staff buy-in.
product_or_system_implication: Low-cost triage checklists can be embedded into paper charts and later digitized.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Brazil's Family Health Strategy: Using Community Health Workers to Provide Primary Care
condition_or_issue: chronic disease, minor illness, social risk, preventive care
topic_category_or_theme: fragmented care; no one connected the dots; documentation error
finding_or_quote_or_case: Community knowledge is powerful but can be under-documented or poorly integrated into clinical records.
what_happened: CHWs provide home basic care, register families, relay information to teams, resolve low-level problems and refer complex problems.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: hidden needs and chronic disease adherence issues; missed: signals may be lost if CHW observations do not reach nurse/physician workflow.
product_or_system_implication: Create structured CHW observation fields and escalation pathways into primary-care team huddles.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: HEARTS in the Americas: Hypertension Clinical Pathways
patient_stage_or_process_step: vitals; labs ordered; lab interpretation; long-term monitoring
condition_or_issue: hypertension and cardiovascular risk; diabetes/CKD integration
topic_category_or_theme: lab/vital trend missed; no follow-up ownership
finding_or_quote_or_case: Screening must connect to protocolized treatment and follow-up, not just identify high BP.
what_happened: PAHO HEARTS clinical pathways standardize treatment protocols, proper BP measurement and cardiovascular risk assessment in PHC.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: hypertension and CVD risk; missed: poor BP technique, lack of treatment intensification or follow-up.
product_or_system_implication: Track BP measurement quality, risk category, protocol step, medication intensification and return date.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Specific health checkup and specific health guidance
patient_stage_or_process_step: before visit; public health screening; long-term monitoring
finding_or_quote_or_case: Preventive flow identifies risk before symptoms but depends on participation and sustained behaviour support.
what_happened: Specific health checkups screen insured people/dependents age 40+; results stratify into information provision, motivational support or proactive support.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: metabolic risk; missed: people outside eligibility/attendance or follow-up lifestyle support.
product_or_system_implication: Link screening results to risk stratum, guidance plan, 3-month review and primary-care referral if needed.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: National Health Screening Program
patient_stage_or_process_step: before visit; public health screening; lab interpretation; follow-up
topic_category_or_theme: abnormal result not followed up; no follow-up ownership
finding_or_quote_or_case: Screening result delivery by mail does not ensure comprehension or completion of confirmation testing.
what_happened: Patient checks eligibility, visits authorized hospital with ID/checkup card, receives results by mail; suspected disease may receive confirmation checkup.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: screen-positive or suspected disease; missed: non-attendance, inaccurate results if pre-test instructions not followed, confirmation follow-up gap.
product_or_system_implication: Use confirmation-test tracking and patient-language result explanations.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Danish Cancer Patient Pathways: three-legged strategy
patient_stage_or_process_step: medical history; labs ordered; referral; specialist review
condition_or_issue: suspected cancer with alarm or nonspecific symptoms
topic_category_or_theme: rare disease delay; referral delay; no one connected the dots
finding_or_quote_or_case: Countries need a route for serious nonspecific symptoms, not only organ-specific red flags.
what_happened: Three-legged strategy: urgent alarm symptoms to organ-specific pathways; serious nonspecific symptoms to tests and diagnostic centres; vague low-risk symptoms managed by GP with diagnostic access.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: cancers without alarm symptoms; missed: vague symptoms if GP access to diagnostics is limited or safety-netting weak.
product_or_system_implication: Create nonspecific-symptom pathways with GP gut-feeling field, blood-panel bundle and diagnostic-centre referral.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Risk of cancer and serious disease in Danish patients with urgent referral for NSSC
condition_or_issue: non-specific symptoms and signs of cancer
topic_category_or_theme: rare disease delay; referral delay; normal-test trap
finding_or_quote_or_case: Nonspecific symptoms can represent high-yield diagnostic risk when systematically routed.
what_happened: Cohort study of urgent referral to Denmark's non-organ-specific cancer pathway; around one fifth had cancer.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: substantial cancer prevalence among non-specific symptom referrals; missed: patients with serious symptoms outside pathway criteria.
product_or_system_implication: Support structured referral for uncertainty, not just classic red flags.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Healthcare Israel
patient_stage_or_process_step: before visit; first clinician assessment; long-term monitoring
topic_category_or_theme: lab trend missed; no one connected the dots
finding_or_quote_or_case: Digitized history reduces fragmentation but still requires pattern recognition and alert design.
what_happened: Israel's HMO-based system begins with family doctor/community health centers and uses longstanding digital records/HIE to give clinicians historical data at point of care.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: strong longitudinal data infrastructure; missed: data availability does not guarantee synthesis of trends or patient narrative.
product_or_system_implication: Use longitudinal problem timelines and trend alerts, not just data access.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Maccabi Healthcare Services: Diagnostic Services
patient_stage_or_process_step: labs ordered; imaging ordered; lab interpretation; follow-up
topic_category_or_theme: abnormal result not followed up; no follow-up ownership
finding_or_quote_or_case: Result delivery is not the same as result action.
what_happened: Diagnostic services are computerized from referral through collection/test and result delivery to physician and user stations; teleradiology centralizes interpretation for distant sites.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: lab/imaging workflow and result dissemination; missed: action on result still depends on clinician/patient follow-up loop.
product_or_system_implication: Link diagnostic-result delivery to acknowledgment and documented clinical next step.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Data analysis reveals common errors that prevent patients from getting timely, accurate diagnoses
patient_stage_or_process_step: labs ordered; imaging ordered; referral; follow-up
topic_category_or_theme: documentation error; poor handoff; abnormal result not followed up
finding_or_quote_or_case: Diagnostic failure is often an operations/communication problem, not just cognitive error.
what_happened: Analysis of >3,000 diagnostic safety events found many errors in the testing process plus monitoring/follow-up and referral/consultation phases.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Missed/delayed: diagnoses due to technical, administrative and communication failures.
product_or_system_implication: Instrument handoffs: order-to-result, result-to-action, referral-to-appointment and missed-appointment loops.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Undiagnosed: When Your Rare Diagnosis Comes 30 Years Late
patient_stage_or_process_step: repeat visit; specialist review; imaging/genetic/special testing
topic_category_or_theme: rare disease delay; patient self-advocacy required; no one connected the dots
finding_or_quote_or_case: Rare disease patients often carry the pattern across disconnected visits themselves.
what_happened: Patient story describes self-advocacy leading to specialist programme review and eventual rare diagnosis after decades.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Missed/delayed: rare diagnosis across many earlier encounters.
product_or_system_implication: Give patients a longitudinal symptom/problem timeline they can share across systems.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Living with CKD: diagnosis experiences
patient_stage_or_process_step: medical history; lab interpretation; diagnosis discussion
topic_category_or_theme: chronic illness invalidation; lab trend missed; documentation error
finding_or_quote_or_case: Chronic illness identification can fail even when data exists if communication/records are not visible.
what_happened: Advocacy patient stories describe how CKD diagnosis experiences can involve communication problems, dismissed symptoms and records not seen.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Missed/delayed: chronic disease recognition or patient understanding.
product_or_system_implication: Pair lab trend alerts with patient-facing explanations and record visibility checks.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Types and Origins of Diagnostic Errors in Primary Care Settings
patient_stage_or_process_step: first clinician assessment; medical history; physical exam; labs ordered
condition_or_issue: varied common diseases with potential harm
topic_category_or_theme: incomplete history; physical exam missed; repeated visits ignored
finding_or_quote_or_case: The highest-yield diagnostic signals may appear only after a return visit or hospitalization.
what_happened: Study identified primary-care diagnostic errors using EHR triggers such as unplanned hospitalization/return visits/ED visits after index primary-care encounter.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Missed/delayed: common diseases; process breakdowns in patient-practitioner encounter, history, exam and workup ordering.
product_or_system_implication: Use trigger tools to surface unplanned returns after recent visits and prompt diagnostic review.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Emergency Severity Index (ESI): A Triage Tool for Emergency Department Care, Version 4
patient_stage_or_process_step: red flag missed; normal-test trap; incomplete history
topic_category_or_theme: red flag missed; normal-test trap; incomplete history
finding_or_quote_or_case: Emergency Severity Index (ESI): A Triage Tool for Emergency Department Care, Version 4
what_happened: Classify acuity using ESI: immediate life-saving intervention, high risk/severe pain, then expected resources, with danger-zone vitals review.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: patients needing immediate lifesaving intervention and high-risk care; risk: triage depends on nurse pattern recognition and local resource assumptions.
product_or_system_implication: Separate emergency risk score from diagnostic uncertainty; capture clinician concern and prior visits even when ESI level is lower.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Diagnostic Errors in the Emergency Department: A Systematic Review
patient_stage_or_process_step: red flag missed; repeated visits ignored; no follow-up ownership
condition_or_issue: stroke, MI, aortic dissection, VTE, meningitis/encephalitis, sepsis, spinal cord injury/compression, lung cancer
topic_category_or_theme: red flag missed; repeated visits ignored; no follow-up ownership
finding_or_quote_or_case: Diagnostic Errors in the Emergency Department: A Systematic Review
what_happened: Systematic review estimated diagnostic error harms and common high-harm conditions.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Missed/delayed: serious ED diagnoses, especially vascular, neurologic, infection and cancer presentations.
product_or_system_implication: Flag high-harm conditions linked to symptom clusters and require explicit exclusion/safety-netting before discharge.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Guidance for emergency departments: initial assessment
patient_stage_or_process_step: poor handoff; red flag missed; no follow-up ownership
topic_category_or_theme: poor handoff; red flag missed; no follow-up ownership
finding_or_quote_or_case: Guidance for emergency departments: initial assessment
what_happened: Initial assessment can include streaming, triage and rapid assessment/treatment to identify sickest patients and direct others to right service.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: sickest patients and appropriate service route; risk: redirection decisions can become unsafe if not clinically governed.
product_or_system_implication: Track real elapsed time from physical arrival to clinical assessment, not just time after registration.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Initial Assessment of Emergency Department Patients
patient_stage_or_process_step: arrival; registration; red flag missed; poor handoff
topic_category_or_theme: arrival; registration; red flag missed; poor handoff
finding_or_quote_or_case: Initial Assessment of Emergency Department Patients
what_happened: Register quickly, perform initial assessment by trained registered clinician, record minimum clinical fields and use early warning scores where relevant.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: front-door risk; missed risk if queues are unmanaged before registration/assessment.
product_or_system_implication: Use queue surveillance and arrival-time capture; include deterioration prompts before formal triage is complete.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Emergency Department Workflow Diagrams

topic_category_or_theme: documentation error; no one connected the dots; normal-test trap
finding_or_quote_or_case: Emergency Department Workflow Diagrams
what_happened: Patient arrives by EMS/walk-in; triage/intake RN collects key data; provider orders labs/CXR; CURB-65 and clinical assessment guide admission vs outpatient antibiotics.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: pneumonia severity and disposition; risk: pneumonia mimic or alternative diagnosis may be missed if CDS anchors on CAP.
product_or_system_implication: Expose missing CURB-65 elements and create 'pursue other diagnoses' tracking, not just CAP disposition.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Urgent care self-service

topic_category_or_theme: digital access gap; incomplete history; red flag missed
finding_or_quote_or_case: Urgent care self-service
what_happened: Patient self-checks in on kiosk/tablet, completes symptom triage, and can be prioritised, redirected or referred through service routing.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: rapid symptom-led prioritisation and redirection; risk: digital access, literacy and symptom-description gaps.
product_or_system_implication: Add accessibility fallback, clinician override, real-time deterioration alerts and visibility of prior visits.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Urgent treatment centres: principles and standards

topic_category_or_theme: wrong specialty; poor handoff; no follow-up ownership
finding_or_quote_or_case: Urgent treatment centres: principles and standards
what_happened: UTCs accept booked and walk-in patients; provide basic diagnostics or protocols; share records/post-event messages and refer to ED/specialists when needed.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: conditions manageable outside ED; risk: lack of diagnostic capability or wrong redirection.
product_or_system_implication: Show available diagnostics, escalation criteria and post-visit ownership in the same workflow.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows

patient_stage_or_process_step: red flag missed; patient self-advocacy required
topic_category_or_theme: red flag missed; patient self-advocacy required
finding_or_quote_or_case: When to visit an urgent treatment centre
what_happened: On arrival, patients may have observations by nurse, may wait by acuity not arrival order, and can be assessed/treated onsite or referred; GP is updated afterwards.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: worsening symptoms during wait if patient tells staff; risk: passive reliance on patient to report deterioration.
product_or_system_implication: Provide waiting-room check-ins and explicit 'tell us now if...' symptom prompts in multiple languages.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows

topic_category_or_theme: normal-test trap; lab trend missed; documentation error

what_happened: Initial vitals tailored to age, complaint and status; abnormal vitals reported and repeated.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: physiologic instability; missed: serious disease with normal or incomplete vitals.
product_or_system_implication: Require abnormal-vital acknowledgment, repeat-vital timers and trend comparison across visits.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Red Flags Identification and Intervention Policy for Urgent Care Centers
patient_stage_or_process_step: red flag missed; incomplete history; poor handoff

topic_category_or_theme: red flag missed; incomplete history; poor handoff

what_happened: Registration/intake staff ask reason for visit; red flags trigger immediate rooming and clinician involvement or ED transfer.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: conditions too high-risk for routine urgent care; missed: red flags hidden by vague complaint or poor intake phrasing.
product_or_system_implication: Use plain-language red-flag checklist and escalation button at registration.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: NHS 111 online

topic_category_or_theme: incomplete history; wrong specialty; no one connected the dots
finding_or_quote_or_case: NHS 111 online
what_happened: Patient completes one symptom pathway; service gives advice such as self-care, pharmacist, GP, UTC, ED or callback.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: service disposition; missed: multi-symptom or known-condition context if algorithm is used one symptom at a time.
product_or_system_implication: Allow multi-symptom summaries, 'not captured' text, prior-risk integration and easy escalation to human review.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: NHS 111 online staff information

topic_category_or_theme: wrong specialty; poor handoff; fragmented care

what_happened: Uses structured triage and service directory to recommend ambulance/ED/UTC/out-of-hours GP/GP/pharmacist/self-care outcomes.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: appropriate service destination; missed: directory availability or local pathway mismatches can redirect incorrectly.
product_or_system_implication: Audit outcome-to-arrival completion and failed referral attempts; surface no-capacity conditions.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Digital tools for online consultation in general practice

topic_category_or_theme: digital access gap; incomplete history; no follow-up ownership; chronic illness invalidation
finding_or_quote_or_case: Digital tools for online consultation in general practice
what_happened: Asynchronous online consultations support clinical/admin requests but may be capped or rerouted by practices for safety.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Missed/delayed: safety incidents when digital tools reduce access, distort history or weaken proactive/long-term care.
product_or_system_implication: Create safe fallback channels, complexity flags, remote-to-face-to-face conversion rules and continuity prompts.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Telehealth and Patient Safety
patient_stage_or_process_step: normal-test trap; incomplete physical exam; poor follow-up ownership
condition_or_issue: acute or chronic problems assessed remotely
topic_category_or_theme: normal-test trap; incomplete physical exam; poor follow-up ownership
finding_or_quote_or_case: Telehealth and Patient Safety
what_happened: Telehealth can be safer when embedded in a medical home with records, tests, referrals and follow-up; risk rises when exam and communication are limited.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Missed: diagnostic cues requiring auscultation, private/inaccessible body areas, objective vitals or hands-on exam.
product_or_system_implication: Decision support should classify problems as telehealth-suitable vs requiring in-person exam/labs/imaging.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Modern General Practice Model
patient_stage_or_process_step: incomplete history; wrong specialty; no one connected the dots
topic_category_or_theme: incomplete history; wrong specialty; no one connected the dots
finding_or_quote_or_case: Modern General Practice Model
what_happened: Modern access model directs patients via phone/online/in-person to the right professional first time, using care navigation and demand/capacity management.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: routing need; missed: clinical complexity if navigation lacks enough symptom/risk context.
product_or_system_implication: Separate admin routing from clinical triage; capture escalation flags and repeated contacts.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows

topic_category_or_theme: poor handoff; symptom dismissed; red flag missed

what_happened: Care navigation signposts to appropriate clinician or service; clinical triage by nurse/GP makes early clinical decisions.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: difference between signposting and clinical triage; missed: escalation if administrative navigation substitutes for clinical judgment.
product_or_system_implication: Make role boundaries explicit and route uncertain cases to clinical triage.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows

what_happened: Guideline organizes referral/investigation actions by cancer site, symptom and findings; includes safety netting and diagnostic-process advice.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: cancer suspicion thresholds; missed: patients below threshold or with nonspecific/vague symptoms unless active monitoring occurs.
product_or_system_implication: Add active-monitoring tasks and symptom persistence triggers, not only referral thresholds.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Safety netting

what_happened: Safety-netting gives time-bound follow-up, documents advice, ensures tests/referrals completed and monitors persistent symptoms even after negative tests.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: ongoing risk despite low-risk or negative initial results; missed: no owner for follow-up tasks.
product_or_system_implication: Build safety-net plans with due dates, owner, patient-facing instructions and re-presentation counters.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: GP mythbuster: managing test results in general practice

topic_category_or_theme: abnormal result not followed up; documentation error; no follow-up ownership

what_happened: Practice needs systems for ordering, tracking attendance, return/non-return, review, filing, patient communication and abnormal follow-up.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Missed: abnormal result not acted on, result not returned, no patient communication or clinician absence gap.
product_or_system_implication: Implement closed-loop result tracking, unresolved-result inboxes and backup reviewer rules.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Continuity of care: delayed diagnosis in GP practices

topic_category_or_theme: fragmented care; no one connected the dots; missing family/history context; repeated visits ignored

what_happened: Over 8 months the patient saw many clinicians; eventual GP found spinal lump and sent patient to ED where CT found metastatic cancer.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Missed/delayed: unresolved symptoms and cancer-history context were not surfaced across repeat contacts.
product_or_system_implication: Surface unresolved symptom threads, prior high-risk history and multiple-clinician patterns in the EHR.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Jess's Rule: Three strikes and we rethink

topic_category_or_theme: repeated visits ignored; no one connected the dots; bias or minimization
finding_or_quote_or_case: Jess's Rule: Three strikes and we rethink
what_happened: After three presentations, GP teams are prompted to reflect, review and rethink, consider face-to-face exam, second opinion, tests or referral.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: repeat presentation as a diagnostic signal; missed: if repeat visits are not counted across clinicians/channels.
product_or_system_implication: Create automatic repeat-presentation counters across phone/online/in-person and linked symptoms.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows

topic_category_or_theme: symptom dismissed; chronic illness invalidation; bias or minimization; patient self-advocacy required

what_happened: Survey documented repeated GP visits and A&E attendance before diagnosis; respondents reported dismissal/minimisation.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Missed/delayed: endometriosis diagnosis; patients often required repeated self-advocacy.
product_or_system_implication: Track symptom duration/severity, repeated visits and invalidating language; prompt specialist referral criteria review.

what_was_missed_or_identified: Missed/delayed: follow-up appointments, specialist action, scans/tests; GP sometimes unable to accelerate despite abnormal tests.
product_or_system_implication: Track referral state, wait risk, abnormal-test escalation and patient-visible next step.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows

what_happened: Ombudsman reported repeated failures in scan reading, carrying out scans and follow-up; a cancer case was only identified at a fifth A&E visit after lesion not reported.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Missed/delayed: abnormal scan findings and cancer diagnosis.
delay_or_timeline: example six-week delay; other cases vary
product_or_system_implication: Closed-loop imaging: critical finding acknowledgment, patient notification and escalation if no action documented.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
source_title: Effectiveness of a structured triage system in a resource-limited rural hospital in Uganda
patient_stage_or_process_step: red flag missed; arrival; poor handoff

topic_category_or_theme: red flag missed; arrival; poor handoff
finding_or_quote_or_case: Effectiveness of a structured triage system in a resource-limited rural hospital in Uganda
what_happened: Rural OPD implemented IITT-adapted structured triage using a three-colour checklist on charts and short training; patient flow improved without added resources.
what_was_missed_or_identified: Identified: urgent/emergency cases earlier; missed: undertriage still possible and formal triage requires staff buy-in.

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
topic_category_or_theme: triage downgrade if complex but stable; pre-triage queue; prior visits not visible
finding_or_quote_or_case: Arrival -> registration -> triage RN applies ESI decision A/B/C/D -> waiting/treatment area -> provider assessment -> labs/imaging/consults -> disposition

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
topic_category_or_theme: occult waits before registration; unsafe redirection; poor handoff between services
finding_or_quote_or_case: Arrival -> register -> initial assessment: streaming/triage/RAT -> ED, UTC, SDEC/specialty, or off-site service -> ongoing treatment/discharge

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
topic_category_or_theme: anchoring on CAP; missing alternative diagnoses; missing data elements
finding_or_quote_or_case: Walk-in/EMS -> front desk/triage RN -> intake RN -> provider evaluation -> labwork + chest X-ray -> CAP? -> CURB-65 -> admit vs outpatient antibiotics vs pursue other diagnosis

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
topic_category_or_theme: patients wait by acuity; deterioration relies on patient telling staff; diagnostics vary
finding_or_quote_or_case: Walk-in/booked/111 -> arrival -> nurse observations -> assessed by UTC clinician -> tests if available -> treated/prescribed or referred/escalated -> GP update

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
topic_category_or_theme: digital access/language; symptom nuance; no human observation during form completion
finding_or_quote_or_case: Arrival -> patient self-check-in on tablet/kiosk -> demographic match/NHS number -> guided symptom triage -> prioritise/redirect/refer -> clinician receives triage outcome

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
topic_category_or_theme: one-symptom-at-a-time; complex known conditions; directory mismatch
finding_or_quote_or_case: Before visit -> patient enters age/sex/postcode + main symptom -> NHS Pathways questions -> outcome: 999/ED/UTC/GP/pharmacist/nurse callback/self-care -> receiving service

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
topic_category_or_theme: routing without enough clinical detail; repeated contacts not linked
finding_or_quote_or_case: Patient contacts by phone/online/in-person -> care navigation/admin or clinical triage -> GP/nurse/pharmacist/other professional -> labs/imaging/referral or self-care -> safety-net/follow-up

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
topic_category_or_theme: lost/non-returned results; clinician absence; filing without action
finding_or_quote_or_case: Clinician orders test -> patient attends or not -> result returns or not -> trained staff/clinician review -> patient informed -> abnormal follow-up/referral -> filing/closure

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
topic_category_or_theme: normal-test trap; multiple clinicians; repeat count across channels missing
finding_or_quote_or_case: Initial visit -> low-risk/negative tests -> safety-net plan -> repeat presentation -> third presentation triggers reflect/review/rethink -> exam/peer review/tests/referral -> diagnosis or monitored plan

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
topic_category_or_theme: wrong worklist/status; patient confusion; advice without safety net
finding_or_quote_or_case: GP/referrer creates e-RS referral -> provider RAS triages before booking -> book/redirect/return advice/request more info -> patient appointment or primary-care management -> follow-up

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
topic_category_or_theme: rejected referral; advice-only drift; missing critical context
finding_or_quote_or_case: GP symptom concern -> specialist advice/guidance or straight-to-test -> diagnostic test/consultant review -> advice, appointment or treatment pathway -> safety-net

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
topic_category_or_theme: positive screens not tracked; referral loop not closed; low-resource constraints
finding_or_quote_or_case: Population empanelment -> ASHA/home contact -> HWC screening age 30+ -> CHO/doctor assessment -> referral for suspected NCD/cancer/mental health -> two-way follow-up

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
topic_category_or_theme: undertriage; staff training; paper chart data not aggregated
finding_or_quote_or_case: OPD arrival -> nurse applies IITT-adapted checklist/color category on chart -> emergency/urgent/non-urgent prioritisation -> clinician assessment -> treatment/referral/discharge

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
topic_category_or_theme: stockout; referral transport; lack of counter-referral
finding_or_quote_or_case: Caregiver/CHW contact -> CHW assesses fever/cough/diarrhoea and danger signs -> classifies/treats uncomplicated illness -> refers severe cases -> supervisor/facility follow-up

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
topic_category_or_theme: community signals not structured; no closed loop to clinician
finding_or_quote_or_case: Geographic assignment -> CHW home visits/registers family -> notes symptoms/living conditions/adherence -> resolves low-level issue or relays/refers to nurse/physician -> primary-care follow-up

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
topic_category_or_theme: kit non-return; positive FIT no colonoscopy; false reassurance after negative
finding_or_quote_or_case: Eligibility/GP register -> FIT kit mailed -> patient collects sample -> lab result -> no blood routine recall OR further tests -> specialist nurse -> usually colonoscopy -> result/follow-up

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
topic_category_or_theme: nonattendance; support drop-off; risk not connected to primary care
finding_or_quote_or_case: Insurer eligibility age 40+ -> annual checkup/questionnaire/labs -> risk stratification -> information/motivational/proactive support -> 3+ month evaluation

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
topic_category_or_theme: mail result not understood; confirmation follow-up gap
finding_or_quote_or_case: NHIS eligibility notice -> patient schedules/visits authorized hospital with ID/card -> screening -> results mailed -> confirmation checkup if suspected disease

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
topic_category_or_theme: patients below criteria; vague symptoms managed locally without robust safety net
finding_or_quote_or_case: GP sees symptoms -> alarm symptoms to organ-specific CPP OR serious nonspecific symptoms -> initial tests/blood panel -> diagnostic centre -> cancer/serious disease work-up

package_topic: Research A / health-system patient flows
topic_category_or_theme: result availability without action; alert fatigue; patient notification gap
finding_or_quote_or_case: Family doctor/HMO clinician referral -> collection/imaging site intake -> test performed -> result disseminated to physician computer/user station; teleradiology if distant -> clinician/patient follow-up

Research B / missed signals and diagnostic errors
condition_or_issue: Repeated visits, slow lab drift, weight loss, multi-system symptoms
finding_or_quote_or_case: Longitudinal diagnostic signal accumulator
what_happened: Auto-cluster symptoms/labs/visits/referrals into unresolved diagnostic threads.
product_or_system_implication: Auto-cluster symptoms/labs/visits/referrals into unresolved diagnostic threads.

package_topic: Research B / missed signals and diagnostic errors
condition_or_issue: Return within days/weeks for same/worse complaint
finding_or_quote_or_case: Repeat-visit diagnostic reset
what_happened: Require clinician to review prior diagnosis, what changed, and dangerous alternatives.
product_or_system_implication: Require clinician to review prior diagnosis, what changed, and dangerous alternatives.

package_topic: Research B / missed signals and diagnostic errors
finding_or_quote_or_case: Closed-loop abnormal result ownership
what_happened: Assign owner + deadline + patient notification + acknowledgment + action status.
product_or_system_implication: Assign owner + deadline + patient notification + acknowledgment + action status.

package_topic: Research B / missed signals and diagnostic errors
condition_or_issue: Clinician cites prior/normal test to close diagnosis
finding_or_quote_or_case: Normal-test trap explainer
what_happened: Show test age, scope, symptom status, and what it cannot rule out.
product_or_system_implication: Show test age, scope, symptom status, and what it cannot rule out.

package_topic: Research B / missed signals and diagnostic errors
condition_or_issue: Patient says not being heard, severe impairment, or fear of missed disease
finding_or_quote_or_case: Patient voice + severity capture
what_happened: Structured fields for patient concern, functional limits, photos, family history, and timeline.
product_or_system_implication: Structured fields for patient concern, functional limits, photos, family history, and timeline.

package_topic: Research B / missed signals and diagnostic errors
condition_or_issue: Anxiety/stress/weight/age/gender labels used with persistent physical symptoms
